title: UDLM Accreditation record
description: Machine-validatable Accreditation record (governance/accreditation-and-authorization-matrix.md §3.3). A formal, versioned, time-bounded attestation that a subject (a provider, policy evaluator, credential provider, or peer realization) satisfies a compliance framework — EXPLICITLY scoped to provider + capability + jurisdiction so the sovereignty 1-1 match (UDLM ADR-004 §4; §3.3.1) is machine-checkable. UDLM carries the record; DCM's Governance Matrix consumes it. The `scope.capability_scope` binding grain (provider-wide | category | exact capability version) is org/platform-admin policy.

uuid.description: RFC 9562 v4 identity of this accreditation.

handle.description: Advisory human identity, e.g. accreditations/providers/eu-west-prod-1/fedramp-high. uuid is authoritative.

subject_uuid.description: The subject this attests — the PROVIDER uuid for a service_provider (axis 1 of the sovereignty 1-1 match, §3.3.1).

framework.description: fedramp_high | fedramp_moderate | hipaa | pci_dss_v4 | iso_27001 | soc2_type2 | dod_il4 | dod_il5 | dod_il6 | sovereign | classified | <custom> (open string, §3.3).

scope.description: What this accreditation covers — keyed to provider + capability + jurisdiction so the sovereignty 1-1 match is checkable (§3.3.1). `subject_uuid` names the provider; the fields here name WHICH capabilities and WHICH residency scope it attests.

capability_scope.description: Which of the provider's capabilities this attests, at the platform-configured binding grain (ADR-004 §4). Each entry is grain 1 (provider_wide), grain 2 (category), or grain 3 (exact capability_uuid+version — expires on capability version change).

geographic_scope.description: Residency/jurisdiction this attests (ISO 3166 country / 3166-2 subdivision, or a declared region grouping). Matched per §3.8: RESIDENCY subsumes DOWN a declared jurisdiction hierarchy (an authorization for US covers US-MN residency); a distinct SOVEREIGNTY regime is matched EXACTLY (US does NOT cover US-MN as a state regime).

data_classifications.description: Which data classifications this covers (phi, restricted, sovereign, ...) — one axis of the 1-1 match (§3.3.1): a claim over `sovereign` data is not vouched for by an accreditation scoped to `internal`.

plane.description: WHICH PLANE this attestation covers (§3.8): data = data-plane residency (bytes at rest/in-process); control = control-plane / operator-access / support / telemetry; both. The universal residency leak is the control plane — an accreditation covering only `data` does NOT attest operator access stays in-jurisdiction. Tied to the data_role execution-vs-control split (contracts/data-roles.md).

framework_capabilities.description: Compliance-framework capabilities (data_at_rest, data_in_transit, ...) — a DIFFERENT axis from capability_scope (which is the provider's (verb × domain) categories).

stale_after.description: ISO 8601 duration; max gap before stale_action fires.

proof.description: The ACCREDITOR's cryptographic signature over this record — the accreditation is a Verifiable Credential (W3C VC Data Integrity / VC-JOSE-COSE; the Gaia-X model). DCM VERIFIES this proof + chains `verification_method` to a `trust_anchor` + checks `status`/currency BEFORE it appraises scope (§3.7; the two-gate model, §3.3.1). Absent proof = self_asserted tier: usable at dev/eval, never honored for sovereign/fsi.

type.description: Proof suite, e.g. DataIntegrityProof / JsonWebSignature2020 / eddsa-jcs-2022 (VC Data Integrity), or a JOSE alg for VC-JOSE-COSE.

verification_method.description: The accreditor's signing key/DID (e.g. did:web:accreditor.example#key-1, or an x5c cert reference) — resolved to check the signature.

proof_value.description: The detached signature / JWS over the record's canonical form.

trust_anchor.description: How the accreditor's signing key ROOTS to a recognized authority (§3.7). Verification fails if the chain does not terminate in a trust anchor the platform recognizes — the anti-substitution + issuer-trust requirement (RATS §8.1; W3C VC).

type.description: cab = accredited Conformity Assessment Body; eidas_tsp = eIDAS Trust Service Provider; did_web / x509_chain = key-chain roots; government_registry = a sovereign authority; internal = first-party (dev/eval).

reference.description: The anchor identifier (DID, cert-chain reference, CAB / registry id).

capability_scope_entry.description: One binding-grain entry (§3.3.1). Exactly one of two shapes: {provider_wide:true} attests EVERY capability the provider declares (survives capability changes, coarsest); or {capability_uuid, version?, category?} attests a specific capability — add `version` for the deterministic grain a version change EXPIRES, and `category` to narrow to one (verb × domain) category within the capability.

provider_wide.description: Attests every capability the provider declares; survives capability changes.

capability_uuid.description: The capability this attests (matches provider `capabilities[].capability_uuid`). Without `version` it attests that capability across versions; with `version` it binds the exact version.

version.description: Bind the EXACT capability version — a new version is not covered, so the sovereignty claim reverts to self_asserted until re-attested (the deterministic grain).

category.description: Optional — narrow the attestation to one (verb × domain) category within the capability (e.g. attest only realize_resources/Compute within a multi-category capability).
